NKJV.1: Where do wars and fights come from among you? Do they not come from your desires for pleasure that war in your members?
NKJV.2: You lust and do not have. You murder and covet and cannot obtain. You fight and war. Yet you do not have because you do not ask.
NKJV.3: You ask and do not receive, because you ask amiss, that you may spend it on your pleasures.
NKJV.4: Adulterers and adulteresses! Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
NKJV.5: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who dwells in us yearns jealously”?
NKJV.6: But He gives more grace. Therefore He says:  “God resists the proud, But gives grace to the humble.”
NKJV.7: Therefore submit to God. Resist the devil and he will flee from you.
NKJV.8: Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
NKJV.9: Lament and mourn and weep! Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
NKJV.10: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and He will lift you up.
NKJV.11: Do not speak evil of one another, brethren. He who speaks evil of a brother and judges his brother, speaks evil of the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
NKJV.12: There is one Lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy. Who are you to judge another?
NKJV.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a city, spend a year there, buy and sell, and make a profit”;
NKJV.14: whereas you do not know what will happen tomorrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapor that appears for a little time and then vanishes away.
NKJV.15: Instead you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we shall live and do this or that.”
NKJV.16: But now you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
NKJV.17: Therefore, to him who knows to do good and does not do it, to him it is sin.

ESV.1: What causes quarrels and what causes fights among you? Is it not this, that your passions  are at war within you?
ESV.2: You desire and do not have, so you murder. You covet and cannot obtain, so you fight and quarrel. You do not have, because you do not ask.
ESV.3: You ask and do not receive, because you ask wrongly, to spend it on your passions.
ESV.4: You adulterous people!  Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Therefore whoever wishes to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
ESV.5: Or do you suppose it is to no purpose that the Scripture says, “He yearns jealously over the spirit that he has made to dwell in us”?
ESV.6: But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, “God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.”
ESV.7: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
ESV.8: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
ESV.9: Be wretched and mourn and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
ESV.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.
ESV.11: Do not speak evil against one another, brothers.  The one who speaks against a brother or judges his brother, speaks evil against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
ESV.12: There is only one lawgiver and judge, he who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge your neighbor?
ESV.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go into such and such a town and spend a year there and trade and make a profit” —
ESV.14: yet you do not know what tomorrow will bring. What is your life? For you are a mist that appears for a little time and then vanishes.
ESV.15: Instead you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.”
ESV.16: As it is, you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
ESV.17: So whoever knows the right thing to do and fails to do it, for him it is sin.

NASB.1: What is the source of quarrels and conflicts among you? Is not the source your pleasures that wage war in your members?
NASB.2: You lust and do not have; [so] you commit murder. You are envious and cannot obtain; [so] you fight and quarrel. You do not have because you do not ask.
NASB.3: You ask and do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend [it] on your pleasures.
NASB.4: You adulteresses, do you not know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Therefore whoever wishes to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
NASB.5: Or do you think that the Scripture speaks to no purpose: 'He jealously desires the Spirit which He has made to dwell in us'?
NASB.6: But He gives a greater grace. Therefore [it] says, 'GOD IS OPPOSED TO THE PROUD, BUT GIVES GRACE TO THE HUMBLE.'
NASB.7: Submit therefore to God. Resist the devil and he will flee from you.
NASB.8: Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
NASB.9: Be miserable and mourn and weep; let your laughter be turned into mourning and your joy to gloom.
NASB.10: Humble yourselves in the presence of the Lord, and He will exalt you.
NASB.11: Do not speak against one another, brethren. He who speaks against a brother or judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law; but if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge [of it].
NASB.12: There is [only] one Lawgiver and Judge, the One who is able to save and to destroy; but who are you who judge your neighbor?
NASB.13: Come now, you who say, 'Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a city, and spend a year there and engage in business and make a profit.'
NASB.14: Yet you do not know what your life will be like tomorrow. You are [just] a vapor that appears for a little while and then vanishes away.
NASB.15: Instead, [you ought] to say, 'If the Lord wills, we will live and also do this or that.'
NASB.16: But as it is, you boast in your arrogance; all such boasting is evil.
NASB.17: Therefore, to one who knows [the] right thing to do and does not do it, to him it is sin.

NLT.1: What is causing the quarrels and fights among you? Don’t they come from the evil desires at war within you?
NLT.2: You want what you don’t have, so you scheme and kill to get it. You are jealous of what others have, but you can’t get it, so you fight and wage war to take it away from them. Yet you don’t have what you want because you don’t ask God for it.
NLT.3: And even when you ask, you don’t get it because your motives are all wrong — you want only what will give you pleasure.
NLT.4: You adulterers! Don’t you realize that friendship with the world makes you an enemy of God? I say it again: If you want to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
NLT.5: Do you think the Scriptures have no meaning? They say that God is passionate that the spirit he has placed within us should be faithful to him.
NLT.6:
And he gives grace generously. As the Scriptures say, “God opposes the proud
but gives grace to the humble.”
NLT.7: So humble yourselves before God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
NLT.8: Come close to God, and God will come close to you. Wash your hands, you sinners; purify your hearts, for your loyalty is divided between God and the world.
NLT.9: Let there be tears for what you have done. Let there be sorrow and deep grief. Let there be sadness instead of laughter, and gloom instead of joy.
NLT.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up in honor.
NLT.11: Don’t speak evil against each other, dear brothers and sisters. If you criticize and judge each other, then you are criticizing and judging God’s law. But your job is to obey the law, not to judge whether it applies to you.
NLT.12: God alone, who gave the law, is the Judge. He alone has the power to save or to destroy. So what right do you have to judge your neighbor?
NLT.13: Look here, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we are going to a certain town and will stay there a year. We will do business there and make a profit.”
NLT.14: How do you know what your life will be like tomorrow? Your life is like the morning fog — it’s here a little while, then it’s gone.
NLT.15: What you ought to say is, “If the Lord wants us to, we will live and do this or that.”
NLT.16: Otherwise you are boasting about your own pretentious plans, and all such boasting is evil.
NLT.17: Remember, it is sin to know what you ought to do and then not do it.

WEB.1: Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Don’t they come from your pleasures that war in your members?
WEB.2: You lust, and don’t have. You murder and covet, and can’t obtain. You fight and make war. You don’t have, because you don’t ask.
WEB.3: You ask, and don’t receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it for your pleasures.
WEB.4: You adulterers and adulteresses, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
WEB.5: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who lives in us yearns jealously”?
WEB.6: But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”
WEB.7: Be subject therefore to God. But resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
WEB.8: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
WEB.9: Lament, mourn, and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to gloom.
WEB.10: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he will exalt you.
WEB.11: Don’t speak against one another, brothers. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law, but a judge.
WEB.12: Only one is the lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge another?
WEB.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow let’s go into this city, and spend a year there, trade, and make a profit.”
WEB.14: Whereas you don’t know what your life will be like tomorrow. For what is your life? For you are a vapor, that appears for a little time, and then vanishes away.
WEB.15: For you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that.”
WEB.16: But now you glory in your boasting. All such boasting is evil.
WEB.17: To him therefore who knows to do good, and doesn’t do it, to him it is sin.

CSB17.1: What is the source of wars and fights among you? Don’t they come from your passions <sup>ⓛ</sup> that wage war within you? <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.2: You desire and do not have. You murder and covet and cannot obtain. You fight and wage war. <sup>[2]</sup> You do not have because you do not ask.
CSB17.3: You ask and don’t receive because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it on your pleasures. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.4: You adulterous people! <sup>[3]</sup> Don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the friend of the world becomes the enemy of God. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: Or do you think it’s without reason that the Scripture says: The spirit he made to dwell in us envies intensely? <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.6:
But he gives greater grace. Therefore he says: God resists the proud,
but gives grace to the humble. [5] ⓑ
CSB17.7: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.8: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.9: Be miserable and mourn and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.
CSB17.11: Don’t criticize one another, brothers and sisters. Anyone who defames or judges a fellow believer <sup>[6]</sup> defames and judges the law. If you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.12: There is one lawgiver and judge <sup>[7]</sup> who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge your neighbor? <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will travel to such and such a city and spend a year there and do business and make a profit.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.14: Yet you do not know what tomorrow will bring — what your life will be! For you are like vapor that appears for a little while, then vanishes. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.15: Instead, you should say, “If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.”
CSB17.16: But as it is, you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.17: So it is sin to know the good and yet not do it. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: What leads to [the unending] quarrels and conflicts among you? Do they not come from your [hedonistic] desires that wage war in your [bodily] members [fighting for control over you]?
AMP.2: You are jealous and covet [what others have] and your lust goes unfulfilled; so you murder. You are envious and cannot obtain [the object of your envy]; so you fight and battle. You do not have because you do not ask [it of God].
AMP.3: You ask [God for something] and do not receive it, because you ask with wrong motives [out of selfishness or with an unrighteous agenda], so that [when you get what you want] you may spend it on your [hedonistic] desires.
AMP.4: You adulteresses [disloyal sinners—flirting with the world and breaking your vow to God]! Do you not know that being the world's friend [that is, loving the things of the world] is being God's enemy? So whoever chooses to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
AMP.5: Or do you think that the Scripture says to no purpose that the [human] spirit which He has made to dwell in us lusts with envy?
AMP.6: But He gives us more and more grace [through the power of the Holy Spirit to defy sin and live an obedient life that reflects both our faith and our gratitude for our salvation]. Therefore, it says, "God is opposed to the proud and haughty, but [continually] gives [the gift of] grace to the humble [who turn away from self-righteousness]."
AMP.7: So submit to [the authority of] God. Resist the devil [stand firm against him] and he will flee from you.
AMP.8: Come close to God [with a contrite heart] and He will come close to you. Wash your hands, you sinners; and purify your [unfaithful] hearts, you double-minded [people].
AMP.9: Be miserable and grieve and weep [over your sin]. Let your [foolish] laughter be turned to mourning and your [reckless] joy to gloom.
AMP.10: Humble yourselves [with an attitude of repentance and insignificance] in the presence of the Lord, and He will exalt you [He will lift you up, He will give you purpose].
AMP.11: Believers, do not speak against or slander one another. He who speaks [self-righteously] against a brother or judges his brother [hypocritically], speaks against the Law and judges the Law. If you judge the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge of it.
AMP.12: There is only one Lawgiver and Judge, the One who is able to save and to destroy [the one God who has the absolute power of life and death]; but who are you to [hypocritically or self-righteously] pass judgment on your neighbor?
AMP.13: Come now [and pay attention to this], you who say, "Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a city, and spend a year there and carry on our business and make a profit."
AMP.14: Yet you do not know [the least thing] about what may happen in your life tomorrow. [What is secure in your life?] You are merely a vapor [like a puff of smoke or a wisp of steam from a cooking pot] that is visible for a little while and then vanishes [into thin air].
AMP.15: Instead you ought to say, "If the Lord wills, we will live and we will do this or that."
AMP.16: But as it is, you boast [vainly] in your pretension and arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
AMP.17: So any person who knows what is right to do but does not do it, to him it is sin.

MSG.1: Where do you think all these appalling wars and quarrels come from? Do you think they just happen? Think again. They come about because you want your own way, and fight for it deep inside yourselves.
MSG.2: You lust for what you don't have and are willing to kill to get it. You want what isn't yours and will risk violence to get your hands on it. You wouldn't think of just asking God for it, would you?
MSG.3: And why not? Because you know you'd be asking for what you have no right to. You're spoiled children, each wanting your own way.
MSG.4: You're cheating on God. If all you want is your own way, flirting with the world every chance you get, you end up enemies of God and his way.
MSG.5: And do you suppose God doesn't care? The proverb has it that "he's a fiercely jealous lover."
MSG.6: And what he gives in love is far better than anything else you'll find. It's common knowledge that "God goes against the willful proud; God gives grace to the willing humble."
MSG.7: So let God work his will in you. Yell a loud no to the Devil and watch him scamper.
MSG.8: Say a quiet yes to God and he'll be there in no time. Quit dabbling in sin. Purify your inner life. Quit playing the field.
MSG.9: Hit bottom, and cry your eyes out. The fun and games are over. Get serious, really serious.
MSG.10: Get down on your knees before the Master; it's the only way you'll get on your feet.
MSG.11: Don't bad-mouth each other, friends. It's God's Word, his Message, his Royal Rule, that takes a beating in that kind of talk. You're supposed to be honoring the Message, not writing graffiti all over it.
MSG.12: God is in charge of deciding human destiny. Who do you think you are to meddle in the destiny of others?
MSG.13: And now I have a word for you who brashly announce, "Today—at the latest, tomorrow—we're off to such and such a city for the year. We're going to start a business and make a lot of money."
MSG.14: You don't know the first thing about tomorrow. You're nothing but a wisp of fog, catching a brief bit of sun before disappearing.
MSG.15: Instead, make it a habit to say, "If the Master wills it and we're still alive, we'll do this or that."
MSG.16: As it is, you are full of your grandiose selves. All such vaunting self-importance is evil.
MSG.17: In fact, if you know the right thing to do and don't do it, that, for you, is evil.

NIV.1:
Submit Yourselves to God
What causes fights and quarrels among you? Don’t they come from your desires that battle within you?
NIV.2: You want something but don’t get it. You kill and covet, but you cannot have what you want. You quarrel and fight. You do not have, because you do not ask God.
NIV.3: When you ask, you do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend what you get on your pleasures.
NIV.4: You adulterous people, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hatred toward God? Anyone who chooses to be a friend of the world becomes an enemy of God.
NIV.5: Or do you think Scripture says without reason that the spirit he caused to live in us envies intensely?
NIV.6:
But he gives us more grace. That is why Scripture says:
“God opposes the proud
but gives grace to the humble.”
NIV.7: Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
NIV.8: Come near to God and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
NIV.9: Grieve, mourn and wail. Change your laughter to mourning and your joy to gloom.
NIV.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
NIV.11: Brothers, do not slander one another. Anyone who speaks against his brother or judges him speaks against the law and judges it. When you judge the law, you are not keeping it, but sitting in judgment on it.
NIV.12: There is only one Lawgiver and Judge, the one who is able to save and destroy. But you—who are you to judge your neighbor?
NIV.13:
Boasting About Tomorrow
Now listen, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city, spend a year there, carry on business and make money.”
NIV.14: Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.
NIV.15: Instead, you ought to say, “If it is the Lord’s will, we will live and do this or that.”
NIV.16: As it is, you boast and brag. All such boasting is evil.
NIV.17: Anyone, then, who knows the good he ought to do and doesn’t do it, sins.

NRSVCE.1: Those conflicts and disputes among you, where do they come from? Do they not come from your cravings that are at war within you?
NRSVCE.2: You want something and do not have it; so you commit murder. And you covet something and cannot obtain it; so you engage in disputes and conflicts. You do not have, because you do not ask.
NRSVCE.3: You ask and do not receive, because you ask wrongly, in order to spend what you get on your pleasures.
NRSVCE.4: Adulterers! Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Therefore whoever wishes to be a friend of the world becomes an enemy of God.
NRSVCE.5: Or do you suppose that it is for nothing that the scripture says, “God yearns jealously for the spirit that he has made to dwell in us”?
NRSVCE.6:
But he gives all the more grace; therefore it says, “God opposes the proud,
but gives grace to the humble.”
NRSVCE.7: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
NRSVCE.8: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
NRSVCE.9: Lament and mourn and weep. Let your laughter be turned into mourning and your joy into dejection.
NRSVCE.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.
NRSVCE.11: Do not speak evil against one another, brothers and sisters. Whoever speaks evil against another or judges another, speaks evil against the law and judges the law; but if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
NRSVCE.12: There is one lawgiver and judge who is able to save and to destroy. So who, then, are you to judge your neighbor?
NRSVCE.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a town and spend a year there, doing business and making money.”
NRSVCE.14: Yet you do not even know what tomorrow will bring. What is your life? For you are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.
NRSVCE.15: Instead you ought to say, “If the Lord wishes, we will live and do this or that.”
NRSVCE.16: As it is, you boast in your arrogance; all such boasting is evil.
NRSVCE.17: Anyone, then, who knows the right thing to do and fails to do it, commits sin.

NET.1: Where do the conflicts and where do the quarrels among you come from? Is it not from this, from your passions that battle inside you?
NET.2: You desire and you do not have; you murder and envy and you cannot obtain; you quarrel and fight. You do not have because you do not ask;
NET.3: you ask and do not receive because you ask wrongly, so you can spend it on your passions.
NET.4: Adulterers, do you not know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever decides to be the world’s friend makes himself God’s enemy.
NET.5: Or do you think the scripture means nothing when it says, “The spirit that God caused to live within us has an envious yearning”?
NET.6: But he gives greater grace. Therefore it says, “God opposes the proud, but he gives grace to the humble.”
NET.7: So submit to God. But resist the devil and he will flee from you.
NET.8: Draw near to God and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners, and make your hearts pure, you double-minded.
NET.9: Grieve, mourn, and weep. Turn your laughter into mourning and your joy into despair.
NET.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord and he will exalt you.
NET.11: Do not speak against one another, brothers and sisters. He who speaks against a fellow believer or judges a fellow believer speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but its judge.
NET.12: But there is only one who is lawgiver and judge — the one who is able to save and destroy. On the other hand, who are you to judge your neighbor?
NET.13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go into this or that town and spend a year there and do business and make a profit.”
NET.14: You do not know about tomorrow. What is your life like? For you are a puff of smoke that appears for a short time and then vanishes.
NET.15: You ought to say instead, “If the Lord is willing, then we will live and do this or that.”
NET.16: But as it is, you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
NET.17: So whoever knows what is good to do and does not do it is guilty of sin.
